title: Bunny Basket Tote
summary: A small carry basket shaped like a bunny's face and ears, sized for an Easter-basket haul of eggs and candy.
description: This is a jigsaw-and-glue project more than a joinery one: cut a bunny-face silhouette and a matching back panel from plywood, and staple straight fence-picket sides between them to make a basket you can actually carry. The whole thing hinges on getting the traced pattern symmetrical — fold your paper pattern in half before tracing so both ears and both sides of the face match, since any lopsidedness is immediately obvious on a shape this simple and this small.

materials.name: 1x6 boards or fence pickets
materials.note: About 28" total used, for the sides and handle.

cutList.note: Trace a symmetrical bunny-face-and-ears pattern before cutting.
Cut to fit the assembled basket's footprint.

steps.title: Cut the face and back panels
steps.body: Fold a paper pattern in half and trace it so the bunny face and ears come out symmetrical, then transfer the pattern to the 1/2" plywood and cut both the face and back panels with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Attach the sides
steps.body: Glue and staple the two side pieces to the face and back panels, forming the basket's box shape.

steps.title: Attach the bottom and add a handle
steps.body: Cut the bottom to fit and glue and staple it to the sides and front. Add a handle across the top — a strip of the same fence-picket material, or a length of scrap, works well.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand any rough edges, especially anywhere little hands will grip, then stain or paint.

images.alt: A small wooden Easter basket shaped like a bunny face with ears
